game_name: Back to the Future Part II & III

element_introduction_order.mastery_context: 30 items spread across 1955, 1985, 2015 time periods; player must locate and return to proper era
Manage three distinct time periods with items requiring temporal accuracy
Located in optional puzzle room; utility not documented but implied as navigation aid
Single time period (1885) with fewer items and smaller map; simplified challenge compared to Part II

typical_relief_triggers: Item successfully returned to correct time period
typical_tension_triggers: Item location ambiguity across three time periods
Time period mismatch frustration (30 items, 3 periods)
Lack of maps or passwords for guidance
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Exploration-paced collection puzzle; Part II frustration-oriented (no guidance), Part III simplified and streamlined

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Puzzle rooms contain optional items (Compass location)

difficulty_curve.early_game: Part II: High initial difficulty due to 30-item collection requirement across three time periods with no maps
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Part II: Sustained frustration from navigation and item location ambiguity
difficulty_curve.late_game: Part III: Reduced difficulty; simplified map, fewer items, single time period
notable_spikes: Part II introduction: 30-item collection across 3 time periods without guidance
Transition to Part III: difficulty cliff downward

notable_level_patterns.description: Part II spreads 30 collectibles across three distinct time periods (1955, 1985, 2015); player must locate and route items back to their home era
Dedicated optional space containing puzzle mechanics and items (Compass sprite variant suggests world-specific utility)
Part III confines action to one time period (1885) with fewer items and smaller explorable area

sources_used: tcrf (The Cutting Room Floor - Back to the Future Part II & III article)
